focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 3、INT. NORLAND PARK - MR DASHWOOD'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: On his deathbed, Mr. Dashwood reveals to John that Norland estate legally passes to him, leaving his wife and daughters with little; he urges John to help them, who promises, but the dying man's final word is a plea to support them.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Eldest daughter facing financial insecurity after father's death
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Father reveals on his deathbed that inheritance law leaves family with only five hundred pounds a year, and John promises to help
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Family is left with minimal income and must rely on John's promised assistance
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether John will fulfill his promise to help the family
evidence_quotes: Elinor will try to look after you all, but make sure she finds a good husband.
The girls--I have left so little.

relation_updates: Father expresses concern for Elinor's future
status_updates: Becomes financially vulnerable with reduced inheritance
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Practical and concerned eldest daughter, as noted by her father's expectation that she will look after the family.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 7、INT. NORLAND PARK - MRS DASHWOOD'S BEDROOM - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mrs. Dashwood collapses in tears over losing her home; Elinor resolves to find a new house while preparing to endure John's family's arrival.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Daughter taking charge of family relocation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elinor decides to find a new house and prepare for John and Fanny's arrival
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Find a new house for the family
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elinor must start inquiring for housing while awaiting John and Fanny's descent
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Securing a new home before or during the incoming family's stay
evidence_quotes: ELINOR Consider, Mamma! We have nowhere to go.
ELINOR I shall start making inquiries for a new house at once. Until then we must try to bear their coming.

relation_updates: Becomes caretaker for emotionally overwhelmed mother
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Practical decision-maker responding to mother's distress

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Edward impresses Elinor with his thoughtful response about the stables, correcting a mistake Fanny points out, while Marianne and Elinor exchange surprised glances and Mrs. Dashwood smiles warmly.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Observer noting Edward's modest behavior
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Edward's modest correction of his room oversight impresses Elinor, while Fanny reacts angrily
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elinor is impressed by Edward
evidence_quotes: EDWARD has just shaken bands with ELINOR.
MARIANNE and ELINOR look at each other in surprise.
CLOSE on ELINOR. She is impressed.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Perceptive and reserved in interactions

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Edward desires a quiet country life as a clergyman, opposing his mother's ambitions for social status, and expresses disdain for London and the army, preferring simplicity and peace.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Confidante as Edward shares his personal desires and conflicts
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Edward tells Elinor he wants a quiet country life as a clergyman, opposing his mother's ambitions for social distinction
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elinor learns of Edward's preference for a simple life and his family conflict
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Edward's ongoing conflict between his own wishes and his mother's expectations
evidence_quotes: All I want--all I have ever wanted is the quiet of a private life but my mother is determined to see me distinguished.
ELINOR And what do you wish for?

relation_updates: Becomes Edward's confidante about personal struggles
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Elinor listens attentively and asks questions, prompting Edward to elaborate

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elinor reluctantly agrees to go to London as Mrs. Jennings insists, despite initial hesitation; Marianne is thrilled, and Mrs. Dashwood supports the trip, pleased to see her daughter happy.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Bearer of painful secret about Edward's engagement
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Learns Edward is secretly engaged to Lucy Steele and binds herself to secrecy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Stunned by the revelation and committed to keeping the secret
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to interact with Edward while knowing the truth
evidence_quotes: LUCY Edward and I have been secretly engaged these five years.
ELINOR stops walking.
ELINOR I give you my word.

relation_updates: Bound by secrecy pact with Lucy
Relationship with Edward complicated by his engagement
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Discreet and self-sacrificing

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elinor reveals her suppressed anguish over Edward's engagement to Lucy, enduring silent heartbreak while Marianne confronts her, leading to an emotional reconciliation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Elinor reveals her hidden suffering to Marianne after public confirmation of Edward's engagement
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elinor discloses her suppressed anguish over Edward's engagement to Lucy, leading to emotional reconciliation with Marianne
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elinor and Marianne reconciled, preparing to return home
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Edward's engagement to Lucy remains binding
evidence_quotes: MARIANNE bursts into sobs and flings her arms around ELINOR, who, almost impatiently, tries to comfort her.

relation_updates: Marianne learns of Elinor's long-hidden heartbreak
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Maintains composure until emotional outburst, then seeks comfort

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Edward realizes Elinor's role in securing his position, expresses gratitude and affection, but she redirects credit to his merit; they part with emotional restraint, acknowledging his impending marriage to Miss Steele.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Messenger conveying Colonel Brandon's parish offer to Edward
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Delivers the offer, denies personal credit, and formally bids Edward farewell as he prepares to marry Lucy Steele
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To inform Edward of the parish offer and end the meeting appropriately
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Edward leaves to marry Lucy; Elinor stands alone in the room
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Elinor's emotional restraint after parting with Edward
evidence_quotes: They are alone for the first time in months and for a moment, neither speaks.
EDWARD leaves silently. ELINOR stands stock-still in the middle of the room.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Formal and self-effacing in delivering news and concluding the interaction

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 162、INT. CLEVELAND - ELINOR AND MARIANNE'S BEDROOM - LATER
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elinor, overwhelmed with emotion, pleads with her gravely ill sister Marianne not to leave her, expressing her deep love and fear of losing her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Sister pleading with feverish Marianne
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elinor's practical attempt to speak turns into dry sobs as she pleads with Marianne not to leave her alone
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To prevent Marianne from leaving her
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Kneeling by the bed, gulping for breath and kissing Marianne's hand
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Marianne's condition and responsiveness
evidence_quotes: When she speaks, her tone is very practical. ELINOR Marianne, Marianne, please try--
She falls to her knees by the bed

relation_updates: Reveals profound emotional dependence on Marianne
status_updates: Breaks down from composed caretaker to desperate sister
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Deeply loving and dependent on Marianne, shown through emotional breakdown

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marianne reflects on her past imprudence and injustice, especially toward Elinor, embraces personal growth, and resolves to mend her ways through disciplined study.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Sister receiving Marianne's apology and witnessing her commitment to change
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Marianne admits to wronging Elinor and vows to study and improve her behavior
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elinor and Marianne embrace, reconciling their relationship
evidence_quotes: but you--you I wronged above all.
I shall mend my ways!
ELINOR embraces her.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Supportive sister who forgives and embraces Marianne

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Edward reveals he is not married; Lucy married his brother Robert, freeing him. Elinor, overcome with emotion, bursts into tears, leading to a stunned silence before Marianne ushers everyone out.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Hears Edward's news about his broken engagement
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Learns Edward is not married because Lucy married his brother Robert, and cries
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tearful and left alone with Edward after others exit
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether she and Edward will pursue a relationship
evidence_quotes: ELINOR has frozen.
ELINOR rises suddenly,
ELINOR Then you--are not married.
ELINOR bursts into tears.

status_updates: Transitions from composed to emotionally overwhelmed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Shows emotional vulnerability upon unexpected hope

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Edward confesses his enduring love for Elinor, explaining past misunderstandings and declaring his heart has always been hers, as she weeps with emotional relief and joy.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Recipient of Edward's love confession
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Edward declares his heart is and always will be Elinor's, and she responds with tears of released emotion.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elinor is tearful and emotionally affected after Edward's love confession.
evidence_quotes: ELINOR cannot stop crying.
Only to profess, now that I am at liberty to do so, that my heart is and always will be yours.
ELINOR looks at him, her face streaked with tears of released emotion, of pain and of happiness.

relation_updates: Edward declares his heart is Elinor's
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Emotionally relieved with tears showing pain and happiness.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Elinor Dashwood begins as the practical eldest daughter managing her family's financial crisis and relocation after her father's death. She develops a reserved romantic attachment to Edward Ferrars, which is complicated by his secret engagement to Lucy Steele, forcing her to endure personal heartbreak while supporting her sister Marianne through illness and heartbreak. After Edward is freed from his engagement, Elinor's composure gives way to emotional expression, leading to their union and Marianne's recovery.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fanny announces Edward's arrival tomorrow and requests Margaret's room for him to enjoy the view, causing Marianne to react angrily while Elinor remains composed.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Younger sister resentful of Fanny's authority
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Slams her cup down in anger after Fanny requests Margaret's room for Edward.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Visibly angry and resentful toward Fanny's household decision.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Tension over Fanny's control of Norland Park.
evidence_quotes: MARIANNE makes a face.
MARIANNE slams her cup down and throws a furious look at ELINOR.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Impulsive and emotionally expressive

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marianne questions Edward's suitability for Elinor, valuing passionate love over reserve, while Mrs. Dashwood defends Edward's compatibility with Elinor's temperament.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Criticizes Edward's reserve and advocates for passionate love
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Marianne argues that Edward's reserved nature is unsuitable for Elinor, stating that love must be passionate and intense.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Marianne reaffirms her romantic ideals and expresses doubt about finding a man who meets her standards.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Her skepticism about ever encountering a romantic partner who matches her high expectations.
evidence_quotes: But there is something wanting. He is too sedate his reading last night.
To love is to burn to be on fire, all made of passion, of adoration, of sacrifice!
The more I know of the world, the more I am convinced that I shall never see a man whom I can truly love.

relation_updates: Expresses skepticism about Edward's compatibility with Elinor
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Values passionate and fiery love over polite reserve.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margaret seeks help for injured Marianne; a mysterious horseman appears, tends to her ankle, and carries her home, leaving both sisters awestruck.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Injured during a run and rescued by a stranger on horseback
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Trips and injures ankle while running downhill in misty rain; a stranger arrives, examines her ankle, and carries her home
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Being carried home by the stranger after ankle injury
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Identity and intentions of the rescuer
evidence_quotes: MARIANNE runs off down the hill into the heart of the mist.
she trips and sprawls to the ground, letting out a sharp cry of pain.
MARIANNE, transfixed, answers, Only my ankle.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Impulsive (runs off despite warning) and emotionally responsive (transfixed by rescuer)

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 77、EXT. BARTON COTTAGE - GARDEN PATH AND GATE - DUSK
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marianne agrees to a private meeting with Willoughby, who leaves on horseback; she believes he is about to propose.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Agreeing to a private interview with Willoughby
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Marianne agrees to meet Willoughby alone after he formally requests an interview, and she interprets his tone as indicating a proposal.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To attend the private meeting with Willoughby as requested
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Anticipating Willoughby's proposal with shining eyes after he leaves
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Willoughby will propose during the interview
evidence_quotes: Miss Marianne, will you--will you do me the honour of granting me an interview tomorrow--alone?
There is something about his formal tone that makes her feel shy.
MARIANNE looks after him, her eyes shining. He is coming to propose.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Feeling shy due to Willoughby's formal tone and expectant of his proposal

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marianne is heartbroken after Willoughby's abrupt departure; Elinor suspects dishonesty, while Mrs. Dashwood defends him, leading to emotional tension as both mother and daughter grieve in isolation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Heartbroken lover isolating herself
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Marianne refuses her mother's comfort and isolates herself in her bedroom after Willoughby's abrupt departure.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Isolated in her bedroom, sobbing
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Reason for Willoughby's sudden departure
evidence_quotes: MARIANNE Nothing! Please do not ask me questions!
MARIANNE struggles free.
MARIANNE Please let me be!
She runs off upstairs and we hear her bedroom door slamming.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Vulnerable and emotionally overwhelmed

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marianne is devastated as Willoughby coldly rejects her and joins his party; Elinor and Mrs. Jennings help her leave, while Lucy and Robert offer to escort them, amid gossiping onlookers.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Confronts Willoughby at ball and is publicly rejected
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Marianne joyfully approaches Willoughby, but he coldly rejects her by shaking her hand briefly and walking away, leading to her public humiliation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Leaves the ball in emotional distress, supported by Elinor and Mrs. Jennings.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Why Willoughby rejected her
evidence_quotes: MARIANNE rushes towards him with both arms outstretched, her face luminous with joy.
WILLOUGHBY shakes MARIANNE's hand briefly.
MARIANNE almost sinks to her knees.
we are aware that many are surreptitiously watching.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Joyful expectation shattered by public rejection

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marianne, drenched and trembling at the summit, gazes at Combe Magna, reciting Shakespeare's sonnet on love's constancy amid the storm.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Atop the hill in storm, gazing at Combe Magna
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Reaches the hilltop, drenched and trembling, and whispers a Shakespearean sonnet about enduring love while staring at Combe Magna.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Physically drenched and reciting poetry while looking at Combe Magna.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The significance of Combe Magna as a lost home
evidence_quotes: Soaked to the skin, she stands with the storm raging around her
staring at the spires of Combe Magna

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Reciting love poetry in a storm

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 159、INT. CLEVELAND - ELINOR AND MARIANNE'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marianne, delirious with fever, sees her deceased father at the end of her bed, believing the dead come for the dying, while Elinor watches in fear.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Delirious with fever
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Hallucinates her deceased father and says 'The dead are coming for the dying,' showing severe fever-induced delirium.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Still delirious, hallucinating her father, and in a feverish state with cracked lips.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Marianne will recover from her fever.
evidence_quotes: MARIANNE's eyes glitter with the fever.
MARIANNE It is Papa. Papa has come.
MARIANNE Dearest Papa! The dead are coming for the dying.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Feverish with glittering eyes and cracked lips, mentally delirious.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marianne reflects on her past imprudence and injustice, especially toward Elinor, embraces personal growth, and resolves to mend her ways through disciplined study.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Acknowledging past imprudence and injustice, especially toward Elinor, and resolving to change through study
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Marianne admits her behavior was imprudent and unjust, particularly to Elinor, and commits to studying seriously using Colonel Brandon's library.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To read from Colonel Brandon's library for at least six hours a day to improve her learning.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Determined to mend her ways and pursue disciplined study.
evidence_quotes: I compare it with what it ought to have been. I compare it with yours.

relation_updates: Acknowledges wronging Elinor and they embrace
status_updates: Shifts from romantic despair to resolve for self-improvement
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Expressing regret and resolve for self-improvement

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marianne and Colonel Brandon marry; Edward and Elinor attend; Fanny is hit by a coin during the toss; Willoughby watches from afar on a white horse before riding away.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Bride marrying Colonel Brandon
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Marries Colonel Brandon, with a gravity in her joy that makes her seem much older.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Married woman
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Willoughby watches from afar and then rides away, a reminder of past attachments.
evidence_quotes: The bride and groom appear: MARIANNE, in white lawn, and COLONEL BRANDON in full uniform.
MARIANNE also looks extremely happy - but there is a gravity to her joy that makes her seem much older.
It is WILLOUGHBY. As we draw back further still, he slowly pulls the horse around and moves off in the opposite direction.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Happy with a mature gravity

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Marianne begins as a resentful and romantically idealistic young woman, meets and falls deeply in love with Willoughby, experiences heartbreak and public rejection, confronts her past during a near-fatal illness, recovers with newfound maturity, and ultimately marries Colonel Brandon, marking her transition into a more tempered and grateful state.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 3、INT. NORLAND PARK - MR DASHWOOD'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: On his deathbed, Mr. Dashwood reveals to John that Norland estate legally passes to him, leaving his wife and daughters with little; he urges John to help them, who promises, but the dying man's final word is a plea to support them.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Widow left with three daughters and minimal income
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Becomes a widow when Mr Dashwood dies, and is aware the family has little income, as he stated 'The girls--I have left so little'
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Widowed with three daughters, reliant on John's promised help, with only 500 pounds a year income
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether John will fulfill his deathbed promise to help them
evidence_quotes: MRS DASHWOOD is just managing to conceal her fear and grief
She passes JOHN DASHWOOD as he enters, presses his hand, but cannot speak.
MRS DASHWOOD We expect him at any moment, dearest.

relation_updates: Becomes financially dependent on stepson John
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Grieving wife managing fear and grief

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 7、INT. NORLAND PARK - MRS DASHWOOD'S BEDROOM - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mrs. Dashwood collapses in tears over losing her home; Elinor resolves to find a new house while preparing to endure John's family's arrival.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Displaced widow facing loss of home
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mrs Dashwood collapses in tears while packing her belongings, prompting Elinor to resolve to find a new house.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Emotionally overwhelmed and packing, with Elinor taking charge of housing inquiries.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: No new house secured; must endure John and Fanny's arrival.
evidence_quotes: MRS DASHWOOD is rushing about, mourning ribbons flapping, putting her knick-knacks into a small valise.
MRS DASHWOOD To be reduced to the condition of visitor in my own home! It is not to be borne, Elinor!
She suddenly collapses into a chair and bursts into tears.
ELINOR I shall start making inquiries for a new house at once. Until then we must try to bear their coming.

relation_updates: Reliance on Elinor for practical solutions
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Grieving widow struggling with displacement.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Fanny warns Mrs. Dashwood that Edward's compassion makes him vulnerable to imprudent marriage, threatening his inheritance; Mrs. Dashwood is outraged by the implication and leaves.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Target of Fanny's warning about Edward's marriage prospects and inheritance
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mrs. Dashwood hears Fanny state that Edward will lose financial support if he marries without fortune, realizing this threatens a match with Elinor.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Appalled and furious, she ends the conversation by leaving.
evidence_quotes: It is impossible for MRS DASHWOOD not to get the point. She is appalled and furious.
MRS DASHWOOD I understand you perfectly. She sweeps off.

relation_updates: Relationship with Fanny becomes openly adversarial after understanding the warning
status_updates: Now aware of the financial consequences if Edward marries Elinor

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mrs. Dashwood insists on moving soon to Barton Cottage; Edward expresses concern, is invited to visit, but Fanny reminds him of obligations to their mother in town.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Matriarch announcing family relocation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Announces the family's immediate move to Barton Cottage, stating they must leave as soon as possible due to no longer trespassing on Fanny's goodwill.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Family is set to relocate to Barton Cottage immediately.
evidence_quotes: MRS DASHWOOD My cousin Sir John Middleton has offered us a small house on his estate.
MRS DASHWOOD Oh, my dear Edward, we can no longer trespass upon your sister's good will. We must leave as soon as possible.
MRS DASHWOOD ignores FANNY.

relation_updates: Invites Edward to visit Barton Cottage
Ignores Fanny's reminder of Edward's obligations
status_updates: Family set to relocate from Norland Park to Barton Cottage
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Matriarch who announces the move and invites Edward while ignoring Fanny's reminder.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margaret finds the Jennnings amusing, while Mrs. Dashwood and Elinor are overwhelmed by their excessive generosity and abrupt departure.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Arriving at Barton Cottage and greeted by Sir John Middleton and Mrs Jennings
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Arrives exhausted, is welcomed by the hosts, and shows strain from their excessive generosity
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To settle into the cottage and respond to the hosts' social expectations
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Fatigued and strained from the journey and welcome, still engaged with the hosts
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Dealing with the overwhelming generosity of Sir John and Mrs Jennings
evidence_quotes: As the exhausted DASHWOODS alight,
The strain of exhibiting joy and gratitude is beginning to tell on MRS DASHWOOD who is sagging visibly.

relation_updates: Establishes positive social bond with Sir John Middleton
Establishes positive social bond with Mrs Jennings
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Polite but overwhelmed matriarch

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A stranger named John Willoughby of Allenham rescues someone, then rides off, leaving Mrs. Dashwood excited and grateful.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mother asking for rescuer's identity
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Learns the rescuer is John Willoughby and shows excitement
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Excited and grateful after hearing Willoughby's name
evidence_quotes: MRS DASHWOOD calls out after him.
MRS DASHWOOD Please tell us to whom we are so much obliged?
THE STRANGER John Willoughby of Allenham--your servant, ma'am!
CLOSE on MRS DASHWOOD's excited expression.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Willoughby, avoiding further emotional strain, abruptly leaves Barton Cottage without a clear explanation, deepening the tension and disappointment among the Dashwoods.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mrs Dashwood as hostess and mother inquiring about Willoughby's sudden departure
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Willoughby announces he is being sent to London by Lady Allen, avoids stating when he will return, and leaves the cottage abruptly
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To learn Willoughby's return plans and reaffirm his welcome at Barton Cottage
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Disappointed and concerned after Willoughby's evasive and hurried exit
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The reason for Willoughby's sudden departure and his future intentions regarding the family
evidence_quotes: MRS DASHWOOD Willoughby! What is the matter?
MRS DASHWOOD How very disappointing! But your business will not detain you from us for long, I hope?
MRS DASHWOOD For shame, Willoughby! Can you wait for an invitation from Barton Cottage?

relation_updates: Willoughby's relationship with family becomes strained and distant
status_updates: Family's social connection with Willoughby abruptly severed

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Marianne is heartbroken after Willoughby's abrupt departure; Elinor suspects dishonesty, while Mrs. Dashwood defends him, leading to emotional tension as both mother and daughter grieve in isolation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mother who defends Willoughby's character during a family argument
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mrs Dashwood argues with Elinor to defend Willoughby, then leaves the room distressed and isolates herself in her bedroom
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To uphold Willoughby's good reputation against Elinor's suspicions
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mrs Dashwood is in her bedroom, distressed and separated from her daughters after the argument
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The reason for Willoughby's abrupt departure and his true intentions toward Marianne
evidence_quotes: That is unlikely. Perhaps this--Lady Allen--disapproves of his regard for Marianne and has invented an excuse to send him away?
What are you saying, Elinor? That he has been acting a part to your sister for all this time?
I prefer to give him the benefit of my good opinion. He deserves no less. From all of us.
MRS DASHWOOD goes into her bedroom and shuts the door.

relation_updates: Tension with Elinor over Willoughby's character
status_updates: Distressed and isolated after defending Willoughby

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elinor reluctantly agrees to go to London as Mrs. Jennings insists, despite initial hesitation; Marianne is thrilled, and Mrs. Dashwood supports the trip, pleased to see her daughter happy.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mother who consents to daughters' trip to London
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mrs Dashwood agrees to let Elinor and Marianne go to London with Mrs. Jennings, motivated by seeing Marianne smile again
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To see Marianne happy, as indicated by her relief at Marianne's smile
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mrs Dashwood remains at Barton Cottage while her daughters prepare to go to London
evidence_quotes: MRS DASHWOOD You know perfectly well you are too young, dearest.
MRS DASHWOOD, who is both delighted and relieved to see MARIANNE with a smile on her face.
MRS DASHWOOD Of course I can!

status_updates: Consents to separation from daughters for an extended period

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elinor rushes to her mother, relieved, announcing that the person in danger is now safe as Brandon and Mrs. Dashwood arrive.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mother arriving with Colonel Brandon to receive news of Marianne's recovery
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Arrives at Cleveland and is immediately told by Elinor that Marianne is out of danger
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mrs Dashwood has arrived and been informed that Marianne is out of danger
evidence_quotes: BRANDON and MRS DASHWOOD enter
practically swoons into her mother's arms.
ELINOR Mamma! She is out of danger!

relation_updates: Reunites with Elinor in a moment of shared relief
status_updates: Marianne's health status changes to out of danger
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Relieved mother upon hearing good news

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elinor and Mrs. Dashwood reflect on neglect and emotional pain, acknowledging Elinor's hidden struggles and the weight of impending hardship.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mother acknowledging her neglect of Elinor during a garden conversation
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mrs. Dashwood confesses to Elinor that she and the family have neglected her and commits to observing her more closely
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To observe Elinor more closely to understand her hidden struggles
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Mrs. Dashwood has admitted her neglect and expressed intent to be more attentive, with Elinor sharing a painful reflection and receiving physical reassurance
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Mrs. Dashwood can effectively perceive Elinor's concealed feelings despite her commitment
evidence_quotes: Your father once told me not to allow you to neglect yourself. Now I find that it is I who have neglected you most.
Yes, I have. We all have. Marianne is right.
Then we must observe you more closely.
MRS DASHWOOD squeezes ELINOR's arm tightly.

relation_updates: Acknowledges her own failure to attend to Elinor's needs
Commits to more attentive observation of Elinor

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Edward reveals he is not married; Lucy married his brother Robert, freeing him. Elinor, overcome with emotion, bursts into tears, leading to a stunned silence before Marianne ushers everyone out.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mother hosting Edward and learning he is not married
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Learns from Edward that he is single because Lucy married his brother Robert
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Informed of Edward's freed status, with Elinor crying and the family ushered out of the room
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The potential future for Elinor and Edward's relationship
evidence_quotes: MRS DASHWOOD I meant to enquire after Mrs Edward Ferrars.
EDWARD Then you have not heard--the news--I think you mean my brother--you mean Mrs Robert Ferrars.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Mrs. Dashwood begins as a widow facing financial ruin and displacement from Norland Park. She moves her family to Barton Cottage, where she manages social obligations, approves of Willoughby for Marianne, and later endures crises including Willoughby's betrayal and Marianne's illness. Ultimately, she acknowledges her neglect of Elinor and learns of Edward's freed status, pointing toward a resolution for her daughters' futures.
